news.summary: An innovative energy company based in Youngstown, Ohio, has decided to expand its operations by investing in cutting-edge large-format additive manufacturing systems. The investment will involve significant resources and technology to leverage the potential of additive manufacturing to revolutionize the energetics industry.

news.summary: Last week, the S&P400 index, tracking mid-cap stocks, dropped by 9.1%, with a 4.8% decrease noted on Friday. Despite this decline, certain mid-cap stocks performed well. Corcept Therapeutics Incorporated (NASDAQ: CORT) saw a significant increase of 36.28%. The surge followed the company's announcement regarding the success of the ROSELLA Phase 3 trial for relacorilant plus nab-paclitaxel in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer patients. The trial showed improved progression-free survival, as verified by blinded independent central review (PFS-BICR). For more details, check out the full story on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: S&P 400 index dropped 9.1% last week, falling 4.8% on Friday. However, mid-cap stocks like Corcept Therapeutics surged 36.28%.

news.summary: In the latest update, it was reported that a total of 1,245 new COVID-19 cases have been confirmed, bringing the cumulative number of cases to 78,932 in the country. Unfortunately, there were also 37 additional deaths, increasing the total number of fatalities to 3,621. The healthcare system is currently under strain as hospitalizations continue to rise, with 948 patients in serious condition. Authorities are urging the public to adhere to safety guidelines and get vaccinated to help curb the spread of the virus.

news.summary: Aavegotchi, a non-fungible token (NFT) protocol, has decided to shift from blockchain network Polygon to Base, an Ethereum layer-2 scaling chain, following a community vote where 93.5% voted in favor of the proposal. This move reflects Aavegotchi's adaptation to the crypto market downturn and recent challenges. Aavegotchi's developer, Pixelcraft Studios, has made team cuts due to the market conditions. Polygon's total value locked (TVL) has decreased to approximately $725 million as of April 8, compared to highs of nearly $10 billion in 2021, while Base and Arbitrum have each surpassed $2 billion in TVL. Aavegotchi's migration highlights Polygon's struggles in retaining users and TVL amid competition. The decision was influenced by Base's performance and retail onboarding, with its optimistic rollup being favored for these reasons. Aavegotchi, a collaboration between Pixelcraft Studios and Aave, offers NFTs as customizable digital collectibles.
news.headline: Aavegotchi votes 93.5% to switch from Polygon to Base, tackling market downturn and TVL decline on April 8.

news.summary: In New York, Ease Capital is transforming the field of multifamily underwriting by combining artificial intelligence with exclusive market insights and key data partners like CRED iQ. This innovative method aims to improve accuracy in underwriting, simplify decision-making processes, and provide quicker, more efficient investment options for their broker, investor, and multifamily property sponsor clientele. By working alongside CRED iQ, Ease Capital is able to capitalize on cutting-edge techniques.
news.headline: Ease Capital is using AI & data from CRED iQ to modernize multifamily underwriting, aiming for faster, better investments.

news.headline: US trade officials, including Jamieson Greer, indicate willingness for tariff discussions as tariffs loom.

news.summary: A company based in San Francisco, GitLab Inc., specializing in AI-driven DevSecOps solutions, has recently been honored with the 2025 Google Cloud Technology Partner of the Year Award in the Application Development - DevOps segment. This marks the fifth consecutive year that GitLab has received such recognition. The company has strengthened its collaboration with Google Cloud over the past year, introducing numerous product enhancements. Notable achievements include expanded platform integrations aimed at enhancing the workflow efficiency for developers.

news.summary: The price of Ether (ETH), the cryptocurrency for Ethereum, has dropped by 65% in the past three months, leading to concerns about a potential market bottom. Analysts have observed similarities between the current price action and fractal patterns from 2018 and 2022, with indications of bearish divergence and weakening momentum. The current setup suggests a potential further decline towards the $990 - $1,240 price range, aligned with key Fibonacci retracement levels. Additionally, Ethereum's Net Unrealized Profit/Loss (NUPL) has entered the 'capitulation' zone, a sign that most investors are holding ETH at a loss, similar to previous market bottoms. This data, alongside technical analysis, points towards a potential price stabilization near $1,000. The article encourages readers to conduct their own research and emphasizes that all investment decisions involve risks.
news.headline: ETH price near $1,000, possible final bottom. Bullish fatigue seen after 65% fall. Fractals show potential drop to $990 - $1,240 range. NUPL in 'capitulation'. Not investment advice. Conduct own research before decisions.

news.summary: Bitcoin's price has dropped by 5.6% over the past week, closing below the $80,000 support for the first time since Nov. 9, 2024. Despite this decline, futures volume for Bitcoin has surged by 64% during the same period, suggesting increased market activity. However, the open interest in Bitcoin futures contracts has decreased by 19% in the past two weeks, indicating that some traders are closing their positions to lock in profits or manage risks in a bearish market. Total market liquidations have reached $2 billion between April 6 and April 8, indicating traders are taking a cautious approach. The combination of rising futures volume and decreasing open interest suggests a transitionary state for Bitcoin, possibly signaling the end of a correction phase and the start of an accumulation period. If Bitcoin's price fails to recover alongside futures volume and open interest, it could signal the beginning of a bear market. On the other hand, if Bitcoin's price rises with open interest and trading volumes, it could signify an accumulation period followed by a potential uptrend. Despite major US equities being down over 20% from their all-time highs, spot Bitcoin ETF outflows have been minimal, suggesting institutional investors are viewing Bitcoin as a hedge or retaining confidence in its long-term value amidst traditional market turmoil.
news.headline: Bitcoin's price drops 5.6% in a week, closing below $80,000 support for the 1st time since Nov. 9, 2024. Futures volume surged 64% during that period, OI declined by 19%. Total crypto liquidations hit $2B from April 6-8, indicating a possible transitionary phase in Bitcoin market. Spot BTC ETF outflows stay low despite major losses in US equities.

news.summary: BlackRock, the world's largest investment firm with $11.6 trillion in assets under management, is partnering with Anchorage Digital for crypto custody services to meet the growing demand from retail and institutional investors. BlackRock holds $45.3 billion in Bitcoin and $1.7 billion in Ether, making it one of the largest providers of crypto exchange-traded products. Anchorage, the only federally chartered crypto bank in the US, will offer custody services and access to digital asset staking and settlement for BlackRock. A $2 billion tokenized fund called BUIDL, backed by US Treasurys, is supported by Anchorage. BlackRock uses Coinbase for custody of Bitcoin in its iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF. Bitcoin funds have seen significant inflows, but 2025 has been volatile, with sharp inflows and outflows. BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF has outperformed competitors, attracting a net inflow of $39 billion. BlackRock has also launched a crypto ETP in Europe.
news.headline: BlackRock partners with Anchorage Digital for crypto custody. BlackRock has $11.6T assets. Holds $45.3B BTC, $1.7B ETH. Source: Arkham.

news.summary: In the latest data report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has reached a record low of 3.8%, with over 200,000 new jobs being added in the past month. The stock market also saw a significant boost with the major indices all showing gains of over 2%. Additionally, the GDP growth for the last quarter was reported at 4.2%, exceeding expectations and marking a strong performance for the economy. Consumer spending has also increased by 5%, indicating a rise in confidence and financial stability among the population. Overall, the economic indicators point towards a robust and thriving market environment.

news.summary: In Schaumburg, Illinois, ISACA has announced updates to the exams and review materials for the Certified Data Privacy Solutions Engineer (CDPSE) and Certified in Risk and Information Systems Control (CRISC) credentials. The new CDPSE exam will launch on June 2, 2025, with preparation materials becoming available from April 2, 2025. For the CRISC exam, the updated version will be released on November 3, 2025. These changes align with the organization's commitment to staying relevant and up-to-date in the evolving risk and privacy professions.
news.headline: ISACA updates CDPSE & CRISC exams: New exam for CDPSE on 2 June 2025, CRISC on 3 Nov 2025 with updated review materials.

news.summary: Fortinet has been commended for its efforts in enhancing cloud security by providing integrated and scalable solutions designed specifically for Google Cloud customers.The recognition reflects Fortinet's commitment to offering advanced cloud security solutions tailored to meet the unique needs of Google Cloud users. This convergence represents a crucial support area to monitor for potential developments. More on this available on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: In Los Altos, California, Afero, a prominent IoT platform, declared that Bret Jordan, its Chief Security Strategist, has been designated as the Chair of a United Nations (UN) global security standards working party. Jordan will be instrumental in developing essential international security standards for cutting-edge technologies like IoT, smart cities, mobile technologies, autonomous vehicles, and others. This appointment highlights Afero's significant role in shaping the future of global security standards.
news.headline: Bret Jordan of Afero appointed Chair of UN security standards working party, shaping global standards for IoT and emerging tech.
